topic: A person accidentally sends a text to the wrong recipient
raw_output:
[story 1]

My thumb hovered over the screen, a moment of pure, unadulterated panic washing over me. I’d meant to send that detailed itinerary to Sarah, the one who was flying to Tokyo next week. Instead, a string of overly enthusiastic vacation plans, complete with inside jokes only I understood, had zipped out to my boss, Mr. Henderson. I stared at the notification bubble, my jaw tightening. It was 9:03 AM on a Tuesday. I could practically feel the heat rising in my neck. I snatched my phone back, my fingers trembling slightly as I navigated to the messaging app to try and delete the message, a futile gesture that only seemed to amplify my mounting frustration.

[story 2]

"Seriously?" I muttered under my breath, the sound barely audible above the hum of the office air conditioning. I had triple-checked the contact name before hitting send—Sarah’s name, definitely. But the message, a rambling confession about my weekend plans, had landed squarely in the inbox of Brenda from Accounting. I slammed my palm flat onto the desk, the cheap veneer vibrating slightly beneath my grip. My eyes darted between the screen and the closed office door, a tight knot forming in my stomach. I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to force my features into a neutral mask, but the slight tremor in my hand betrayed the internal turmoil. This was just… impossible.

[story 3]

I leaned back in my chair, letting the silence of the cubicle farm wash over me, a slow burn starting in my chest. The text was sent. It was undeniably sent. The recipient was clearly not the intended party; the tone was utterly inappropriate for the recipient's professional life. I rubbed my temples vigorously, the pressure throbbing behind my eyes. Every second stretched into an agonizing crawl. I picked up the phone again, my movements jerky and uncoordinated, searching desperately for an undo button that simply didn't exist. A low groan escaped my lips as I stared blankly at the glowing screen, utterly defeated by the sheer, ridiculous malfunction of my own fingers.
